Overview
- 
Epidermolysis bullosa
Contents 
Overview 
Symptoms 
Treatment 
Epidermolysis bullosa (EB) is the name for a group of rare inherited skin disorders that cause the skin to become very fragile. Any trauma or friction to the skin can cause painful blisters. 
Symptoms of epidermolysis bullosa 
Common symptoms in all types of EB include:
skin that blisters easily blisters on the hands and soles of the feet thickened skin that may be scarred or change colour over time thickening of the skin and nails 
Credit: SCIENCE PHOTO LIBRARY https://www.sciencephoto.com/media/151100/view
Credit: SCIENCE PHOTO LIBRARY https://www.sciencephoto.com/media/877183/view
Types of epidermolysis bullosa 
The 3 main types of EB are:
epidermolysis bullosa simplex (EBS) – the most common type, which can range from mild, with a low risk of serious complications, to severe dystrophic epidermolysis bullosa (DEB) – which can range from mild to severe junctional epidermolysis bullosa (JEB) – a rare form of EB that ranges from moderate to severe The type reflects where on the body the blistering takes place and which layer of skin is affected.
